---
title: "Weekly Student Report"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
output:
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
params:
  student_id: ""
  week_id:    ""
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE,
                      fig.width = 7, fig.height = 4)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

ANALYTICS_HOME <- Sys.getenv("ANALYTICS_HOME", ".")
source(file.path(ANALYTICS_HOME, "config.R"))
source(file.path(ANALYTICS_HOME, "scripts", "utils.R"))
source(file.path(ANALYTICS_HOME, "analysis", "weekly_student_analysis.R"))
source(file.path(ANALYTICS_HOME, "analysis", "weekly_trend_analysis.R"))
source(file.path(ANALYTICS_HOME, "scripts", "fetch_student_snapshots.R"))
source(file.path(ANALYTICS_HOME, "scripts", "fetch_data.R"))

student_id <- params$student_id
week_id    <- params$week_id

stats   <- weekly_student_analysis(student_id, week_id)
trends  <- weekly_trend_analysis(student_id, num_weeks = 8)
student <- fetch_student(student_id)
wp      <- fetch_weekly_period(week_id)

snaps <- with_connection(function(con) {
  dbGetQuery(con, sqlInterpolate(con,
    "SELECT ses.*, ls.actual_start
       FROM student_emotion_snapshots ses
       JOIN lecture_sessions ls ON ls.id = ses.session_id
       JOIN weekly_periods wp ON wp.id = ?wid
      WHERE ses.student_id = ?sid
        AND ses.captured_at BETWEEN wp.start_date
            AND DATE_ADD(wp.end_date, INTERVAL 1 DAY)
      ORDER BY ses.captured_at",
    wid = week_id, sid = student_id))
})
```

---

**Student:** `r paste(student$first_name, student$last_name)` (`r student$student_number`)  
**Programme:** `r student$program`  
**Week:** `r wp$week_number` / `r wp$year` (`r wp$start_date` – `r wp$end_date`)

---

## 1. Overall Stats

```{r overall-stats}
tbl <- data.frame(
  Metric = c("Sessions Attended", "Total Snapshots",
             "Avg Concentration", "Attentive %",
             "Drowsy Episodes", "Confusion Duration (min)",
             "Dominant Emotion"),
  Value  = c(stats$sessions_attended,
             stats$n_snapshots,
             ifelse(is.na(stats$avg_concentration), "N/A",
                    sprintf("%.1f / 100", stats$avg_concentration)),
             ifelse(is.na(stats$attentive_percentage), "N/A",
                    pct(stats$attentive_percentage)),
             stats$drowsy_episodes,
             round(stats$confusion_duration, 1),
             ifelse(is.na(stats$dominant_emotion), "N/A",
                    stats$dominant_emotion)),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
knitr::kable(tbl, col.names = c("Metric", "Value"))
```

## 2. Concentration Timeline

```{r conc-timeline}
if (nrow(snaps) > 0) {
  snaps_plot <- snaps %>%
    mutate(
      captured_at = as.POSIXct(captured_at),
      conc_score  = concentration_to_score(concentration)
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(conc_score)) %>%
    arrange(captured_at)

  ggplot(snaps_plot, aes(x = captured_at, y = conc_score)) +
    geom_line(colour = "#2196F3", linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.3, se = FALSE,
                colour = "#0D47A1", linewidth = 1.2) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 25)) +
    labs(x = "Time", y = "Concentration Score (0-100)",
         title = "Concentration Over the Week") +
    theme_minimal()
} else {
  cat("No snapshot data available for this week.")
}
```

## 3. Emotion Breakdown

```{r emotion-pie}
if (nrow(snaps) > 0) {
  emo_tbl <- snaps %>%
    count(emotion) %>%
    mutate(pct = n / sum(n))

  ggplot(emo_tbl, aes(x = reorder(emotion, -n), y = n, fill = emotion)) +
    geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
    scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
    labs(x = "Emotion", y = "Count",
         title = "Emotion Distribution This Week") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))
} else {
  cat("No emotion data available.")
}
```

## 4. Comparison to Class Average

```{r class-comparison}
class_avg <- with_connection(function(con) {
  dbGetQuery(con, sqlInterpolate(con,
    "SELECT AVG(CASE WHEN concentration='high'   THEN 85
                     WHEN concentration='medium' THEN 55
                     WHEN concentration='low'    THEN 25
                     ELSE 10 END) AS avg_class_conc
       FROM student_emotion_snapshots ses
       JOIN lecture_sessions ls ON ls.id = ses.session_id
       JOIN weekly_periods wp ON wp.id = ?wid
      WHERE ses.captured_at BETWEEN wp.start_date
            AND DATE_ADD(wp.end_date, INTERVAL 1 DAY)",
    wid = week_id))
})

comp_df <- data.frame(
  Group = c("This Student", "Class Average"),
  Conc  = c(stats$avg_concentration,
            as.numeric(class_avg$avg_class_conc))
)

ggplot(comp_df, aes(x = Group, y = Conc, fill = Group)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#42A5F5", "#EF5350")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +
  labs(y = "Avg Concentration Score", x = "",
       title = "Student vs Class Average Concentration") +
  theme_minimal()
```

## 5. Multi-Week Trend

```{r trend}
if (nrow(trends$weekly_stats) >= 2) {
  ggplot(trends$weekly_stats,
         aes(x = as.integer(week_number), y = avg_concentration)) +
    geom_line(colour = "#4CAF50", linewidth = 1) +
    geom_point(colour = "#4CAF50", size = 2) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, colour = "#1B5E20",
                linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8) +
    labs(x = "Week Number", y = "Avg Concentration",
         title = sprintf("8-Week Trend (%s)", trends$trend_direction)) +
    theme_minimal()
} else {
  cat("Insufficient data for trend analysis.")
}
```

## 6. Recommendations

```{r recs}
cat(recommend_from_concentration(stats$avg_concentration))
if (!is.na(stats$drowsy_episodes) && stats$drowsy_episodes > 3) {
  cat("\n• Repeated drowsiness detected. Consider discussing sleep habits.")
}
if (!is.na(stats$confusion_duration) && stats$confusion_duration > 15) {
  cat("\n• Extended confusion episodes. Academic support may be beneficial.")
}
if (identical(trends$trend_direction, "declining")) {
  cat("\n• Concentration trend is declining. Early intervention recommended.")
}
```
